description: Optional SessionEnd hook that runs /retro after non-trivial sessions. Off by default — copy to your ~/.claude/hooks/ or <project>/.claude/hooks/ to enable.

hooks.command: bash -c 'tokens=$(wc -w < "$CLAUDE_TRANSCRIPT_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "${tokens:-0}" -gt 1000 ]; then echo "Session was non-trivial ($tokens words). Run /retro to extract learnings."; fi'

notes.enable-instructions: This hook only prints a reminder. To actually invoke /retro automatically, replace the command with a way to invoke the slash command in your client. For Claude Code CLI, this is currently a manual user action — the hook serves as a nudge.
notes.off-by-default-rationale: Auto-running /retro on every session end would burn tokens for trivial sessions. The user opts in by copying this file.
